module_mood_contract: grounded competence — 'I can feel when a plan is good before approving it' (M2 opener primer; resolves into the exercise, not in the lecture)

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Meta-frame lectures are closers; this is an opener-as-primer.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Maintainer block line 56: 'primer-before-exercise. Names the three moves... Does NOT name plan-mode approval inflation — that label lands retroactively at exercise P5.' Opener arms, doesn't name.
10-12 min within M2 budget. Three-things + three-pressures shape is direct-descriptive; no practitioner-attribution borrowed from the closer.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood contract named; mood-floor 8+/10 on teaching beats.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Phase mood 8, close 8. Maintainer block line 57 names mood target. Engineer voice, operational opener.
Lecture frames pressures as cognitive traps ('structure is persuasive', 'reasonableness passes for rightness'), not as student failure.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Lead with discipline, not failure mode.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Body lead is 'Three things a good plan HAS' — positive discipline. Pressures section follows.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategy-fidelity — no front-running downstream teaching moments.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lecture does NOT name 'plan-mode approval inflation' (held for exercise P5 retro per strategy doc M2 detail). Inlines push-back as 'what you do with this' (line 39) but does not name Pocock skill — skill reveal held for M3.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body teaches the work; trainer manages the room. No authored conversation cues.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No 'compare with the person next to you', 'share with the room', 'discuss with...' shapes. Body is about the work (plan reading) throughout.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing function lives in prompts, not body prose.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lecture is a primer; forcing function lives downstream in the M2 exercise. The optional prompt ({{prompt:when-a-plan-is-good-1}} at line 19) is correctly framed as arming ('Skip if you trust the framing'), not as forcing function.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Action lead-in before any prompt fence.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Line 17: 'Ask Claude to describe what changed in its behaviour when plan mode turned on.' — clean action lead-in immediately preceding line 19 fence.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Module-to-module arc — close hands off cleanly.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lines 38-46 telegraph exercise shape (two push-backs, walk-down, approve, no execute) without spoiling the retro label. Final paragraph: 'After you've done it once, you'll feel when a plan needs the second read' — felt-evidence lands in exercise, not lecture.

Lines 7-9 land the M2 discipline-not-toggle frame in paragraph one: 'Plan mode in Claude Code is a permission state, not a feature... The read-only part is load-bearing. Plan mode isn't "Claude thinks before doing." It's "Claude writes a thing you can read, edit, and push back on before anything changes."' Matches strategy doc M2 refusal of 'plan mode as a feature tour.'
